caption:
The late afternoon sun had faded into a golden hour glow that filtered through the dusty blinds of the Literature Department's private office. I adjusted the hem of my cream silk blouse, feeling the cool fabric slide against my skin as I turned to face my latest student, who hadn't stopped fidgeting since he walked in. My name is Elena, and this was my favorite part of the job—after hours, when the lecture hall emptied and the real work began. 🕯️

"You're the only one here, Professor," he said, his voice low, eyes dropping to my cleavage before snapping back up to my dark, unreadable eyes. His heart was racing, and I could see the pulse in his neck.

I smiled, the curve of my smile designed to distract without promising anything immediately. "And you're the only one who stayed late to ask about the thesis," I replied, walking closer to the mahogany desk. The pencil skirt I wore hugged my hips, the silk blouse hugging my curves, perfectly hiding the fire beneath but promising it to those who knew where to look. 👠

The air between us grew thick, charged with the kind of electricity only a few of my students ever experienced. I let my hands rest on the edge of the desk, my nails clicking softly against the wood. The scent of old books and vanilla perfume lingered in the room, mingling with the heat of anticipation.

"Tell me what you really want," I whispered, leaning forward, the silk shifting with a soft rustle as I stretched slightly, accentuating the silhouette my outfit was designed to create.

He stood up, closing the gap, his breath warm against my ear. "I don't know, Professor," he breathed, his hand grazing my thigh, sending a shiver through my silk-clad body. The fabric whispered against the leather of his pants.

I laughed softly, a low, dangerous sound. "Then let me show you what knowledge looks like outside the classroom. 📚 Tonight, the lecture isn't about grammar or syntax. It's about passion."

He swallowed hard, his gaze lingering on the curve of my waist as he took a step closer. "How do I start?"

"Start by closing the door," I whispered, tapping the wood with a manicured nail. "And then... listen."

#UniversityTeacher #SecretLectures
